heading: Release from Militia duty upon order to active duty in reserve components
label: 8903 Release from Militia duty upon order to active duty in reserve components
text: When ordered to active duty, a member of the Navy Reserve or the Marine Corps Reserve who is a member of the Naval Militia is relieved from all service and duty in the Naval Militia from the date of active duty specified in his orders until he is released from active duty.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)7853.50 U.S.C. 1073, .July 9, 1952, ch. 608, § 50366 Stat. 500The words “in the service of the United States” are omitted as covered by the definition of “active duty” in . The words “is relieved” are substituted for the words “shall stand relieved”.§ 101 of this title
Amendments2018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 7853 of this title2006— substituted “Navy Reserve” for “Naval Reserve”.Pub. L. 109–163

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019
